report_name: Progress Patches Critical Command Injection Flaws in MOVEit WAF and LoadMaster

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-3517
vulnerabilities.description: An authenticated command injection vulnerability in the 'addcountry' API command of Progress ADC products. An attacker with 'Geo Administration' permissions could inject and execute arbitrary OS commands.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-3519
vulnerabilities.description: An authenticated command injection vulnerability in the 'aclcontrol' API command of Progress ADC products. An attacker with 'VS Administration' permissions could inject and execute arbitrary OS commands.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-3518
vulnerabilities.description: An authenticated command injection vulnerability in the 'killsession' API command of Progress LoadMaster. An attacker with 'All' permissions could execute arbitrary OS commands due to unsanitized input.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-4048
vulnerabilities.description: A UI-based authenticated command injection vulnerability in Progress ADC products. An attacker with 'All' permissions could upload a custom WAF rule file containing malicious code, leading to command execution.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-21876
vulnerabilities.description: A WAF policy bypass vulnerability in Progress MOVEit WAF. An attacker could craft a request with a malicious payload in an earlier multipart header to bypass WAF detection, as only the last header was being checked for character set validation.
affected_products: Owasp Owasp Modsecurity Core Rule Set < 3.3.8
Owasp Owasp Modsecurity Core Rule Set < 4.22.0

indicators.name: Progress ADC API Command Injection Attempt
indicators.pattern:
title: Progress ADC API Command Injection Attempt
id: 2b1a9c8d-3e4f-4a5b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects potential command injection attempts against Progress ADC product APIs (addcountry, aclcontrol, killsession) as described in CVE-2026-3517, CVE-2026-3519, and CVE-2026-3518.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/progress-patches-multiple-vulnerabilities-in-moveit-waf-loadmaster/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - cve.2026.3517
  - cve.2026.3519
  - cve.2026.3518
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate API calls with unusual characters in parameters could trigger.
level: high
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection_api:
    c-uri-stem|contains:
      - 'addcountry'
      - 'aclcontrol'
      - 'killsession'
  selection_chars:
    c-uri-query|contains:
      - ';'
      - '|'
      - '&&'
      - '`'
      - '$('
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects potential command injection attempts against Progress ADC product APIs (addcountry, aclcontrol, killsession) by looking for the command names in the URI stem and common injection characters in the query parameters.

indicators.name: Progress ADC Malicious WAF Rule Upload
indicators.pattern:
title: Progress ADC Malicious WAF Rule Upload
id: 8f9a0b1c-2d3e-4f5a-6b7c-8d9e0f1a2b3c
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation or modification of custom WAF rule files on a Progress ADC appliance, which could indicate an attempt to exploit CVE-2026-4048.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/progress-patches-multiple-vulnerabilities-in-moveit-waf-loadmaster/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - cve.2026.4048
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate creation or modification of custom WAF rules by administrators.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|startswith: '/var/airlock/waf/custom-rules/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation or modification of files in the custom WAF rule directory on Progress ADC appliances, a behavior associated with the exploitation of CVE-2026-4048.

indicators.name: Progress WAF Bypass Attempt (CVE-2026-21876)
indicators.pattern:
title: Progress WAF Bypass Attempt (CVE-2026-21876)
id: 5a6b7c8d-9e0f-1a2b-3c4d-5e6f7a8b9c0d
status: experimental
description: Detects HTTP requests with a multipart/form-data content type, which could be part of an attempt to bypass Progress WAF policies as per CVE-2026-21876. The vulnerability involves using multiple multipart headers; this rule detects the presence of the header and requires further analysis.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/progress-patches-multiple-vulnerabilities-in-moveit-waf-loadmaster/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-21
tags:
  - cve.2026.21876
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate applications that use multipart/form-data requests (e.g., file uploads).
level: low
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-content-type: 'multipart/form-data'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects HTTP requests using the 'multipart/form-data' content type, which is a hunting hint for potential exploitation of the WAF bypass vulnerability CVE-2026-21876.
